hero.headline: Louisiana wage, overtime and meal-break law in 2026
hero.subhead: No state minimum wage, no state OT, no adult break mandate. The R.S. 23:632 penalty is where Louisiana gets specific.

secondary_cta.label: When does entity formation make sense?

at_a_glance.penalty_detail: 90 days wages or wages from demand to payment, whichever is less

minimum_wage.question: What is the minimum wage in Louisiana in 2026?
minimum_wage.answer_first: Louisiana's minimum wage is $7.25 per hour, the federal FLSA floor. The state has no state minimum wage law of its own.
minimum_wage.local_preemption_note: 1997 state law prohibits parishes and municipalities from setting a higher local minimum wage
minimum_wage.recent_legislation: HB 353 (proposed $12 by 2027) failed in committee April 2026

overtime.question: Does Louisiana have state overtime rules beyond the FLSA?
overtime.answer_first: No. Louisiana has no state overtime premium. Non-exempt employees get 1.5x their regular rate for hours over 40 per workweek under the FLSA.

meal_breaks.question: Are employers required to provide meal breaks in Louisiana?
meal_breaks.answer_first: No state law requires meal or rest breaks for employees 18 and older in Louisiana. For employees under 18, a 30-minute break is required after every 5 consecutive hours worked under LA R.S. 23:213.

final_paycheck.question: When must an employer pay a final paycheck in Louisiana?
final_paycheck.answer_first: Louisiana R.S. 23:631 requires a discharged employee's final pay within 15 days or the next regular payday, whichever comes first. Resigned employees must be paid by the next regular payday.
final_paycheck.penalty_detail: 90 days wages at daily rate, or wages from date of demand until employer pays, whichever is less
final_paycheck.good_faith_exception_note: Good-faith dispute reduces liability to disputed amount plus judicial interest from suit date
final_paycheck.partnership_exclusion: Profits-interest distributions from federal-partnership-taxed entities excluded from 15-day rule (effective 1 August 2025, R.S. 23:631(F))

enforcement.question: Who enforces wage and overtime law in Louisiana?
enforcement.answer_first: Federal wage and hour enforcement in Louisiana falls to the US Department of Labor Wage and Hour Division (WHD). Louisiana has no state agency dedicated to minimum wage enforcement.
enforcement.federal_agency: US DOL Wage and Hour Division
enforcement.final_paycheck_enforcement: Private lawsuit in Louisiana state court (LA R.S. 23:632)

meta.title: Louisiana Wage, Overtime and Meal-Break Law 2026 | Teamed
meta.description: Louisiana: no state minimum wage (federal $7.25 applies), no state OT premium, no adult meal-break mandate. But R.S. 23:632 penalty — 90 days wages — is real.
